title: Public Prototype Sample
purpose: Reviewed multiple projects from the last six months to identify overlap, consolidation opportunities, and new product directions.
executive_summary: Starter handoff generated from structured notes. Intended to preserve direction and reduce re-research, not replace direct source verification.

findings.title: DashClaw and Practical Systems overlap in client-facing automation, governance,
findings.summary: DashClaw and Practical Systems overlap in client-facing automation, governance, and operational surfaces.

findings.title: The budget-aware research agent is a promising x402 wedge and should remain legi
findings.summary: The budget-aware research agent is a promising x402 wedge and should remain legible as a standalone capability even if folded into a larger platform.

findings.title: TreasuryClaw proved there is value in strong constrained workflows with real art
findings.summary: TreasuryClaw proved there is value in strong constrained workflows with real artifacts and evaluation.

findings.title: Several projects share the same underlying pattern: agent capability plus oversi
findings.summary: Several projects share the same underlying pattern: agent capability plus oversight plus durable artifacts.

recommendations.action: Merge overlapping governance and operational concepts into DashClaw v3.
recommendations.why: Captured from the opportunities section of the source notes.
recommendations.risk: Needs direct validation against current repo state.

recommendations.action: Treat Practical Systems as service delivery and DashClaw as platform substrate.

recommendations.action: Preserve reusable artifacts across sessions to reduce re-research cost.

assumptions_to_continue_from: DashClaw and Practical Systems overlap in client-facing automation, governance, and operational surfaces.
The budget-aware research agent is a promising x402 wedge and should remain legible as a standalone capability even if folded into a larger platform.
TreasuryClaw proved there is value in strong constrained workflows with real artifacts and evaluation.
Several projects share the same underlying pattern: agent capability plus oversight plus durable artifacts.
must_reverify: Which projects should remain branded separately versus folded inward?
What is the right boundary between product, consulting layer, and internal operating system?
